title: Production Agent Loop and Goal Capstone · Independent Harness Trial
summary: Build a safe goal-driven agent for a realistic repository or operations task. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a production-ready agent-loop reference implementation with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose workflow or agent patterns and justify complexity from task uncertainty.
Connect persistent goals, adaptive plans, tool state, and terminal conditions.
Integrate permissions, idempotency, stop controls, and fault recovery.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Operations Defense applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend architecture, limitations, cost, monitoring, and operator procedures.
